title: Best VPN for Privacy in 2026: No-Log Providers That Actually Deliver
description: The best VPNs for privacy in 2026 have audited no-log policies, strong jurisdiction, and proven track records. Here are the ones worth trusting.
content:
Why VPN jurisdiction matters for privacy

Where a VPN company is headquartered determines what laws apply to their data. A VPN based in the US is subject to National Security Letters and can be compelled to hand over data without telling users. A VPN based in Panama (NordVPN), the British Virgin Islands (ExpressVPN), or Switzerland (ProtonVPN) operates under different legal frameworks with stronger privacy protections.

Jurisdiction alone is not enough. The VPN must also have a verified no-log policy. That means they do not store any connection logs, IP addresses, or activity data. The only way to trust this claim is if the VPN has been independently audited and the audit results are public.

ProtonVPN: the strongest privacy case

ProtonVPN is based in Switzerland and built by the same team behind ProtonMail. Switzerland has some of the strongest privacy laws in Europe and is outside EU and US jurisdiction. ProtonVPN is open-source, has been independently audited multiple times, and publishes its audit reports publicly. For users who genuinely prioritize privacy, ProtonVPN is the most defensible choice. Price: free tier available, paid plans from around 4 EUR per month.

Mullvad VPN: anonymous by design

Mullvad is the most privacy-focused VPN commercially available. They accept cash and cryptocurrency payments, do not require an email address to sign up, assign random account numbers instead of usernames, and retain zero logs. Their no-log policy has been confirmed by a real-world test: Swedish police raided a Mullvad server in 2023 and found nothing to seize. The server was seized but contained no user data. Mullvad is based in Sweden. Price: flat 5 EUR per month, no discounts, no trials.

NordVPN: audited logs policy with wider features

NordVPN is based in Panama and has had its no-log policy independently audited by PricewaterhouseCoopers and Deloitte. They were also raided in 2018 and no user data was exposed. NordVPN offers a broader feature set than Mullvad or ProtonVPN: double VPN, Tor over VPN, and split tunneling. Speed is generally faster than ProtonVPN. For users who want privacy alongside features, NordVPN is a strong choice. Price: from around 3.50 EUR per month on multi-year plans.

ExpressVPN: historically strong but acquisition concerns

ExpressVPN has a strong privacy reputation but was acquired by Kape Technologies in 2021, raising some concerns about corporate ownership. Their no-log policy has been independently audited. Based in the British Virgin Islands. Still a solid choice, but users with high privacy requirements may prefer ProtonVPN or Mullvad given the ownership change.

What to avoid

Free VPNs. The business model of a free VPN is almost always data monetization. They log your activity and sell it to advertisers. The privacy gain is negative compared to not using one at all. Also avoid VPNs that have never been audited or those headquartered in Five Eyes countries (US, UK, Canada, Australia, New Zealand) if your threat model includes government surveillance.

Our recommendation

Strongest privacy: Mullvad (if you pay cash or crypto and want no trail). Best balance of privacy and usability: ProtonVPN. Best for features alongside privacy: NordVPN.
